data.title: CES 2026 shifts focus to AI product deployment from hardware innovation, sparking industry discussions on global adoption.
data.description: On January 13, 2026 in Las Vegas, discussions at CES focused on artificial intelligence's evolution from hardware advancement to the global uptake of AI-native products. This transition was a key highlight at "CES 2026: Builders," indicating a shift towards addressing the complexities of widespread adoption.

data.title: SEC chair predicts Trump will sign market structure bill; Atkins supports bipartisan legislation for crypto market regulation.
data.description: Paul Atkins, the SEC chair, expressed firm backing for bipartisan legislation designed to shift crypto markets away from a regulatory gray area.

data.title: Tennessee barred from acting against Kalshi: Market can operate freely as judge blocks state regulators due to lawsuit.
data.description: A federal judge has issued a ruling allowing Kalshi to continue its operations in Tennessee without interference from state regulators. This decision came after the prediction market filed a lawsuit against the regulators. For the time being, Kalshi remains unrestricted in the state, pending further legal developments.

data.title: SEC chair questions if US will confiscate Venezuela's claimed $60 billion Bitcoin as analysts can't confirm.
data.description: A number of experts in blockchain technology have reported difficulties in confirming if the Latin American country possesses $60 billion in the form of the digital currency.

data.title: Prof debunks 5 bank myths on stablecoin yields amid ongoing market bill discussions. #ColumbiaBusinessSchool #crypto
data.description: As the market structure bill is set for markups this month, a professor from Columbia Business School has addressed five misconceptions within the banking industry regarding stablecoin yields.

data.title: China's state media highlights US poverty, using "kill line" to show political dominance, distracting from domestic economic issues.
data.description: The official media in China has adopted the gaming term "kill line" to emphasize the country's political dominance over the United States, diverting attention from China's economic struggles. This messaging underscores China's stance on superiority, despite facing economic difficulties internally.

data.title: Recently, ex-NYC mayor introduces 'NYC Token' cryptocurrency post-office, aimed at combating antisemitism.
data.description: The newly launched NYC Token, which initially faced challenges, is now being promoted as a tool in the fight against antisemitism. This initiative, endorsed by the former mayor of New York City, aims to address instances of hatred and discrimination. The cryptocurrency has garnered attention for its unique objective, with a focus on raising awareness and combating antisemitic sentiments. The initiative has resonated with many, receiving significant support as it continues to gain traction in the digital currency space.
